pattern: Flip the DP perspective: instead of asking how many moves suffice for n floors, ask how many floors can be certified in t moves, then binary-search for the minimum t.
hints: Define f(t, k) as the maximum number of floors you can certify with t moves and k eggs. The recurrence is f(t, k) = f(t-1, k-1) + f(t-1, k) + 1 because the dropped floor sits between the break-branch (k-1 eggs, t-1 moves, floors below) and the survive-branch (k eggs, t-1 moves, floors above).
Increase t from 1 upward and compute f(t, k) until it reaches or exceeds n — that first t is the answer. Each round only needs O(k) work with a 1-D update.
Maintain dp[j] = floors certifiable in the current move count with j eggs. Each round: new_dp[j] = dp[j-1] + dp[j] + 1 for j in 1..k. Stop the first round where dp[k] >= n.

edge_inputs.description: one egg, one floor — a single drop always suffices
one egg, must probe every floor in order — linear cost
two eggs, six floors — classic illustration of the approach
abundant eggs but only one floor — always one move regardless of eggs
two eggs on two floors — one move covers both outcomes
maximum eggs and large floor count
one egg, two floors — linear strategy requires two moves

stress.description: random k in [1,20] and n in [1,500] covering the interesting middle regime
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 20)
    n = rng.randint(1, 500)
    return [k, n]
stress.note: n capped at 500 so the O(k*n*log(n)) oracle stays fast; the optimal O(k*log(n)) handles n=10000 easily

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def superEggDrop(self, k: int, n: int) -> int:
        # f(t, k) = max floors certifiable in t moves with k eggs
        # recurrence: f(t,k) = f(t-1,k-1) + f(t-1,k) + 1
        # find minimum t such that f(t, k) >= n
        dp = [0] * (k + 1)
        t = 0
        while dp[k] < n:
            t += 1
            new_dp = [0] * (k + 1)
            for j in range(1, k + 1):
                new_dp[j] = dp[j - 1] + dp[j] + 1
            dp = new_dp
        return t
